instruction:
A chemical factory produces two types of products, $\mathrm{A}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{A}_{2}$. It is known that to manufacture 10,000 bottles of product $\mathrm{A}_{1}$ requires 5 kg of raw material $B_{1}$, 300 kg of $B_{2}$, and 12 kg of $B_{3}$, resulting in a profit of 8,000 yuan. To manufacture 10,000 bottles of $\mathrm{A}_{2}$, 3 kg of raw material $B_{1}$, 80 kg of $B_{2}$, and 4 kg of $B_{3}$ are needed, generating a profit of 3,000 yuan. The factory currently has 500 kg of raw material $B_{1}$, 20,000 kg of $B_{2}$, and 900 kg of $B_{3}$. Under these circumstances, how much of $\mathrm{A}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{A}_{2}$ should the factory produce to maximize profit?

Options:
A. $$
x_{1}=70,x_{2}=60.
$$
B. $$
x_{1}=80,x_{2}=30.
$$
C. $$
x_{1}=40,x_{2}=100.
$$
D. $$
x_{1}=100,x_{2}=0.
$$
E. $$
x_{1}=20,x_{2}=160.
$$
F. $$
x_{1}=0,x_{2}=200.
$$
G. $$
x_{1}=10,x_{2}=170.
$$
H. $$
x_{1}=50,x_{2}=50.
$$
I. $$
x_{1}=30,x_{2}=120.
$$
J. $$
x_{1}=60,x_{2}=70.
$$
